UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CHRISTINA SUTTON'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED IN PART WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED RULE IN PART. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FOLLOWING CAUSES OF ACTION: NEGLIGENCE, WARRANTY OF QUIET ENJOYMENT, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND FRAUD. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RENT ORDINANCE. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: B. PAREDES, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CHRISTINA SUTTON's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED IN PART WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AND OVERRULED RULE IN PART. The Court overrules the demurrer as to the following causes of action: negligence, warranty of quiet enjoyment, negligent infliction of emotional distress, and intentional infliction of emotional distress, and fraud. The demurrer is sustained as to the claim for violation of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. Order is signed in open court. Judge Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: B. Paredes, Not reported (D501)
